project_overview.project_name: Digital ID for Inclusion and Services Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To establish an inclusive digital ID ecosystem and improve service delivery for registered persons in Ethiopia
project_overview.total_financing: US$350 million (SDR 266.3 million equivalent - US$300 million IDA Credit + US$50 million WHR Grant)
project_overview.beneficiaries: At least 90 million Ethiopian nationals and residents, including refugees, asylum seekers, stateless persons, and host communities

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - implementation in conflict-affected areas including northern Ethiopia, with ongoing conflicts in Amhara, Gambela, and Oromia regions

key_components.description: Supporting stakeholder engagement, establishing Digital ID Institution and Data Protection Commission

key_components.component_name: Establishing scalable and secure Fayda ICT infrastructure
key_components.description: Developing Fayda software, data infrastructure, and strengthening digital security
Mass registration and issuance of physical and digital IDs to 90 million people including refugees and host communities
Integration of Fayda authentication into priority sectors and developing Ethiopia Digital Stack
Supporting PMU establishment and project implementation coordination

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Prime Minister's Office (PMO) through National ID Program (NIDP), transitioning to permanent ID Institution within 2 years
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee co-chaired by Minister of Finance and Minister of Cabinet Affairs; Technical Committee chaired by NIDP with multi-stakeholder participation
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers, using STEP system for all procurement transactions
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PMU responsible for M&E with real-time dashboards, quarterly IFRs, satisfaction surveys, and formal annual/midterm reports

identified_risks.risk_description: Ongoing civil conflicts could create low trust in government intentions for Fayda rollout and possible opposition
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Heavy investment in communications and stakeholder engagement; data minimization principles; exclusion of ethnicity/religion data collection

identified_risks.risk_description: High debt distress, foreign currency shortage, parallel exchange market affecting specialized imports
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Early publication of RFPs, international open bids, direct payment methods, priority designation for project items

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited experience managing World Bank projects, salary ceiling constraints for skilled consultants
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Leveraging firm contracts where possible, continued advocacy for policy change, special attention to inter-agency coordination

identified_risks.risk_description: No institutional experience with World Bank projects, weak internal audit capacity, procurement capacity limitations
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Recruiting dedicated procurement specialists, pre-prepared bidding documents, training on World Bank procedures

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential exclusion of marginalized groups, data breaches, safety risks in conflict areas, ethnic/religious tensions
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Inclusive registration processes, data protection provisions, security risk assessment, comprehensive ESMF and SEP

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - multi-channel GRM including toll-free hotline, SMS, online portal, physical help desks, and mobile app functionality
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: 21% gender gap in current Kebele ID coverage; project targets 50% women/girls registration; tailored communication strategies for women

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed to avoid collecting ethnicity/religion data that could fuel discrimination; includes conflict analysis and end-user surveys; special protocols for conflict-affected areas
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia classified as fragile state with ongoing conflicts in northern regions; 3.4 million IDPs; ethnic and religious tensions; limited state presence in remote areas
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security risk assessment and management plan required; safety risks for registration workers in conflict areas; coordination with security forces
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Ethiopia hosts nearly 1 million refugees (3rd largest in Africa); 3.4 million IDPs; project specifically targets refugee inclusion with US$50 million WHR funding
